title: Wildlife & Animal Tracks
subject: Early Life Science & Biology
description: Discover woodland animals, what they eat, and how to spot their footprints in mud and snow.
learningObjectives: Identify common wild animals (rabbits, deer, squirrels, birds).
Recognize basic animal footprints in dirt and snow.
Understand how animals find shelter in winter.

lessons.title: Who Lives in the Forest?
lessons.content: Squirrels build nests of leaves high up in oak trees. Rabbits dig burrows underground. Birds weave nests out of twigs and feathers. Every creature has a home!

lessons.title: Detecting Footprints
lessons.content: When soil is wet or snow is fresh, animal feet leave impressions! Deer leave split heart-shaped hoof prints. Birds leave small three-toed fork shapes.

flashcards.definition: A hole or tunnel dug by a small animal as a home.
Footprints left behind in mud, sand, or snow.
An animal that eats plants, leaves, and berries.

questions.question: Which animal leaves a split heart-shaped hoof print in the mud?
Where do squirrels build their leafy nests?

practicalChallenge.title: Mud & Snow Track Hunter
practicalChallenge.instruction: Walk near a garden or damp path. Find at least 1 footprint left by an animal or bird. Draw it on paper!
